name: Glacier Hills
description_short: Member-owned credit union founded in 1934 serving 13 Wisconsin counties with auto loans, mortgages, personal loans, and NCUA-insured savings products across 5,500+ shared branch locations.
description_long:
Glacier Hills Credit Union was established in 1934 by employees of West Bend Aluminum Company to provide affordable financial access to workers. Over nine decades, it has evolved into a full-service financial cooperative while maintaining its original mission of improving members' financial well-being through not-for-profit operations. The institution is member-owned and governed by cooperative principles, meaning members benefit from ownership stakes and patronage dividends.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. These include auto loans (shown at 4.74% APR for a $35,000 vehicle), boat and RV financing (7.99% APR example), mortgages, personal loans (10.99% APR example), share savings accounts, and certificates of deposit (3.85% APY example). Members also access credit cards, young adult banking tools, and loan calculators. The institution operates through a shared branching network of 5,500+ locations and maintains 5,000+ free ATMs, providing access far beyond its physical footprint.

Glacier Hills differentiates itself through its cooperative structure, which theoretically delivers lower fees and better rates than for-profit banks over a member's lifetime. The organization actively invests in member education and community support, including an annual scholarship program and virtual annual meetings. Membership is open to individuals living or working in 13 Wisconsin counties (Calumet, Columbia, Dodge, Fond du Lac, Green Lake, Jefferson, Milwaukee, Ozaukee, Sheboygan, Washington, Waushara, Waukesha, and Winnebago) plus their immediate families, as well as businesses in those counties.

As a credit union, deposits are federally insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) up to $250,000, providing the same safety as FDIC-insured banks. However, the website provides limited transparency on specific membership requirements, eligibility verification processes, or detailed fee schedules. The example rates shown are representative but actual rates depend on creditworthiness and loan terms. Like most credit unions, Glacier Hills serves a defined geographic and occupational field of membership.
services: Auto loans (vehicles, motorcycles, RVs, boats, powersports, golf carts)
Share savings accounts and certificates of deposit
Young adult banking products and tools
Financial calculators (auto, boat, loan, savings planning)
pros: NCUA federal deposit insurance up to $250,000 on all deposit accounts
Access to 5,500+ shared branch locations and 5,000+ free ATMs through credit union network
Not-for-profit structure designed to return benefits to members through better rates and lower fees
Founded in 1934 with 90+ years of operational history and community presence in Wisconsin
Offers auto loans, mortgages, personal loans, and investment products under one institution
Annual scholarship program available to members and their families
Virtual annual meeting model providing transparent governance to member-owners
cons: Membership limited to 13 specific Wisconsin counties plus immediate family—geographic restriction limits accessibility
Website lacks detailed fee schedule, term disclosures, and specific eligibility requirements for membership verification
Limited information on credit card offerings, rewards programs, or terms compared to national credit card issuers
Example rates provided are not guaranteed and require individual credit qualification
No online account opening process visible; application flow not clearly explained on website
best_for: Wisconsin residents or workers in the 13-county service area seeking lower-cost borrowing and savings alternatives
Auto and boat buyers looking for competitive rates through a member-owned cooperative structure
Small business owners within the service area needing business lending and deposit products
Members prioritizing relationship banking, community connection, and not-for-profit governance over digital-only banking
diagnosis: Glacier Hills is best for Wisconsin residents or workers in its 13-county service area who want NCUA-insured deposits and competitive lending rates through member-owned cooperative ownership. The primary caveat is geographic restriction—membership requires living or working in specific Wisconsin counties, making it inaccessible to most U.S. consumers outside that region.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Glacier Hills — Credit Union in WEST BEND, WI | CreditDoc
meta_description: Glacier Hills — NCUA-insured credit union in WEST BEND, WI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
